body.title: Inference routing aborts the tier on a model hard-error instead of falling through
body.description:
Found 2026-06-19 wiring DS free inference. When the preferred flat-rate model raised a HARD error mid-dispatch (e.g. Gemini 404 'model no longer available', or 'Google API key not set'), InferenceDevice.dispatch RAISED and aborted the whole worker/designer tier instead of falling through to the next candidate (devstral-small-2:24b / qwen3-coder-next on ollama_cloud, which were healthy). Rate-limit (429) IS handled — ping()→False→routing skips the source. But a hard error during the actual completion call is not caught for fall-through. With slugs now correct this is latent, but a future model retirement / transient hard error would silently break a tier.

**Affected files:** devices/inference/device.py (dispatch fall-through), devices/inference/rules_engine.py
**Design rules:** inference-proxy-only, graceful-degradation
**Scope boundary:** IN: on a candidate model hard-error, log and advance to the next routing rule rather than raise. OUT: changing the preference ordering.
**Completion criteria:** with the top candidate forced to hard-error, dispatch returns a completion from the next healthy candidate (not an exception).
**Test plan:** unit — mock top candidate to raise, assert dispatch falls through and completes.
